sprint: Honour World-Owned Object Commitments + Accurate Object Strip Thumbnails
task: A design-sprint feature answering two direct questions: does Creator actually honour World Builder's per-object permission block (moveable/editable/decorationSlot), and can the Object Strip show an object's real content instead of a generic icon. Investigated first via three parallel research passes, then implemented per an approved plan. Object Strip thumbnails: renderer/slideRenderer.js's _pushLayerObject now forwards a normalized visual descriptor (colour swatch, real image, real drawn shape, real glyph, or a text snippet) for every World-owned object, reflecting any live Story-Author override; js/objectStrip.js renders it directly, falling back to the old generic icon when absent. Moveable: _pushLayerObject's locked flag now follows the real authored moveable value instead of being hardcoded true, engaging the exact same drag machinery Cover/Hook/End elements already use; discovered during implementation that js/app.js's existing drag path already reads/writes the generic elementOverrides bag for any element id, making a second override bag unnecessary -- renderer/slideRenderer.js's three draw functions now resolve a Story-Author position override from that same bag before falling back to the authored position, a pure translation that stays byte-identical when no override exists. Editable (in-place content edit only, no delete, per explicit product decision): js/sceneEngine.js gained one generic setContentOverride helper; js/contextPanel.js's World-object disclosure now shows a real colour input, Replace Image button, or Words textarea depending on the object's own kind, writing through that override and redrawing live with no panel rebuild. Decoration Slot (shipped in this same sprint per explicit direction): tools/world-builder-v2/js/services/builder.js's convergeSceneLayer now carries the previously-discarded layer.decorationSlot flag into the compiled package; a flagged object's disclosure gains an Add-your-own-decoration button reusing the existing Sticker Studio flow end to end, seeded to place the very next sticker near the object's own position rather than dead-center. Verified end-to-end via a new Playwright fixture covering all four scenarios against a real compiled/imported/rendered synthetic World; full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
